summary.verdict: CLEAN — 0 new-in-diff cleanliness issues across all 19 changed files

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex identified 6 issues with the draft findings: (1) false positive on lifecycle-baseline-import.sh as archive candidate — it is task-required with functional references and modified <7 days; (2) rule mismatch — the script filename does not match Rule 4 one-time script patterns; (3) scope drift — logs/*.log files not in changed-files list should not appear in findings; (4) count inconsistency — 19 files scanned, not 20; (5) wording inconsistency in item 9 about lifecycle-baseline-import.sh references; (6) summary severity was wrong given the false positive removal.

findings.issue: False positive: lifecycle-baseline-import.sh flagged as archive_candidates but is task-required (M2/AC5/AC8), modified <7 days, with functional test references
findings.proposed_fix: Remove the archive_candidates finding. Add no-finding rationale: lifecycle-baseline-import.sh is required by M2/AC5/AC8, modified <7 days ago, and has functional references; safe_to_archive=false.
findings.rationale: Correct — the script is a functional task deliverable, not a cleanup candidate. The Safety Rules explicitly protect files modified <7 days. Rule 4 one-time script patterns (test-*.sh, temp-*.sh, debug-*.sh, etc.) do not match lifecycle-baseline-import.sh.

findings.issue: Rule mismatch: lifecycle-baseline-import.sh does not match Rule 4 explicit one-time-script filename patterns
findings.proposed_fix: Replace the wording with: lifecycle-baseline-import.sh does not match explicit one-time-script cleanup filename patterns; 'one-time idempotent migration' is functional task intent, not a cleanliness cleanup signal.
findings.rationale: Correct — naming a script a 'migration' in its documentation does not make it match the Rule 4 cleanup patterns.

findings.issue: Changed-files scope drift: logs/*.log files are not in the changed-files list and should not appear in findings
findings.proposed_fix: Remove from findings; move to out_of_scope_observations with introduced_in_diff:false and advisory note.
findings.rationale: Correct — in --changed-files mode, only the listed files are scanned. The logs/*.log advisory note is preserved in out_of_scope_observations.

findings.issue: Count inconsistency: the listed paths total 19 files, not 20
findings.proposed_fix: Correct the summary to 19 files scanned.
findings.rationale: Correct — the changed-files input has 19 paths. Updated changed_files_count to 19.

findings.issue: Wording inconsistency: item 9 said lifecycle-baseline-import.sh has doc-only references, conflicting with functional test references
findings.proposed_fix: Rewrite item 9 as: lifecycle-baseline-import.sh is not unreferenced; it is functionally referenced by lifecycle/generated tests and required by the task. Not an archive candidate.
findings.rationale: Correct — the script is referenced by test_AC5 and test_AC8 which invoke it directly.

findings.issue: Summary severity was wrong after removing the false positive
findings.proposed_fix: Change summary to: 0 archive candidates, 0 critical, 0 major, 0 minor new-in-diff cleanliness issues.
findings.rationale: Applied — summary updated to reflect 0 new-in-diff issues.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All 6 findings applied: archive_candidates finding for lifecycle-baseline-import.sh removed (false positive); logs/*.log advisory moved to out_of_scope_observations; file count corrected to 19; no-finding rationale added for lifecycle-baseline-import.sh with functional references explanation; summary updated to 0 new-in-diff issues.
